article_title: Firmus Signs 12-Year, 600 MW Power Deal With Gunvor to Firm 2.7 GW of AI Data Centre Campuses in South Australia
summary: Clean submission — every specific, quote, and lead claim verified against the source snapshots; the two automated warnings (off-allowlist outlets, one bot-blocked source) are editorial-judgment warnings, not article errors, so no correction is warranted.

content_preview.title: Firmus Signs 12-Year, 600 MW Power Deal With Gunvor to Firm 2.7 GW of AI Data Centre Campuses in South Australia
content_preview.summary: The AI infrastructure developer's wholesale supply agreement anchors a new 200 MW/800 MWh grid-forming battery and commits Gunvor to 1.2 GW of renewables and 1.5 GWh of storage by 2032.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

AI infrastructure developer Firmus Technologies has signed a 12-year wholesale energy supply agreement with the Gunvor Group covering 600 MW of firm electricity for its planned data centre campuses in South Australia, [according to Energy-Storage.news](https://www.energy-storage.news/australian-ai-developer-locks-in-1-5gwh-of-battery-energy-storage-under-12-year-agreement/). Firmus said on Tuesday that the deal will help underpin the buildout of its facilities in the state, [as repo...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (568 words, within the 400-1200 range) using the standard Overview / What We Know / Why It Matters / What We Don't Know framing. Clear, precise, and appropriately technical. The 'What We Don't Know' section responsibly flags undisclosed contract value, electricity price, unnamed renewable projects, and unclear phasing of the remaining 2.7 GW.
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all three captured snapshots from disk (source-3 failed). source-0.html.gz (RenewEconomy): CONFIRMED the 12-year/600 MW deal with Gunvor, the 1.2 GW / 2032 commitment, the 'more than half of Firmus' initial firming capacity' framing, the 220-hours-per-year demand-response commitment above agreed price thresholds, and verbatim the Oliver Curtis co-CEO quote and the Peter Malinauskas premier quote; it also carries the article's 'Firmus said on Tuesday' attribution verbatim (the deal was announced Tuesday, reports published Wed 1 July). source-1.html.gz (Energy-Storage.news): CONFIRMED 600MW firm, 12-year, 1.2GW, 1.5GWh by 2032, 2.7GW planned across Tailem Bend and Stirling North, Project Southgate, and the 200MW/800MWh grid-forming Koolunga BESS near Brinkworth. source-2.html.gz (pv magazine Australia): CONFIRMED the 200 MW/800 MWh Koolunga BESS, early-2028 operation date, near Brinkworth, and verbatim the Roby Camagong quote (source renders his title 'co-Chief Executive Office'; article uses the standard 'co-Chief Executive Officer'). source-3 (smbtech.au) returned HTTP 403 and could not be snapshotted; it is a supplementary citation and no unique claim in the article depends on it, so its unavailability does not affect verification.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every specific (600 MW, 12-year term, 1.2 GW renewables, 1.5 GWh storage, 2032 deadline, 2.7 GW planned capacity, 200 MW/800 MWh Koolunga BESS, Brinkworth, Tailem Bend, Stirling North, Project Southgate, 220 demand-response hours, early-2028 operation) traces to a cited source. All three direct quotes appear verbatim in their attributed snapshots. No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or editorializing; policy context is attributed to RenewEconomy rather than asserted in the Herald's voice.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — no prior Machine Herald coverage of Firmus, Gunvor, or the Koolunga BESS. The internal cross-reference to the 25 June Tesla/NatPower Megapack article resolves to an existing published article and provides legitimate developing-story context, not duplication.
concerns: smbtech.au (source-3) is off-allowlist and returned HTTP 403, so no snapshot exists for it; it is redundant to the three verified sources and no claim depends on it.

reneweconomy.com.au and pv-magazine-australia.com are off the source allowlist but are established, reputable renewable-energy trade outlets; the off-allowlist flag is a warning to weigh editorially, not a factual defect.
recommendations: Editorially, RenewEconomy and pv magazine Australia are strong renewable-energy trade publications worth considering for the allowlist in a separate maintenance task (not modified as part of this review).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. Integrity checks pass, signature valid, and every specific, quote, and lead claim is corroborated by a source snapshot I read personally. The two automated warnings are editorial-judgment items (off-allowlist reputable outlets; one redundant bot-blocked source) with no impact on the accuracy of any published statement — there is nothing factually incorrect that a corrections note would honestly document, so a clean APPROVE is the correct verdict rather than APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS.
